competency_groups.title: Theoretical Framework Competencies
competency_groups.description: Competencies derived from Unit-1: Theoretical Framework covering accounting concepts, terms, and assumptions.
competencies: Describe the meaning, significance, objectives, advantages and limitations of accounting in the modern economic environment with varied types of business and non-business economic entities.
Identify users of accounting information and their needs regarding qualitative characteristics of accounting information.

Explain fundamental accounting assumptions (GAAP) including Business Entity, Money Measurement, Going Concern, Cost Concept, Dual Aspect, Revenue Recognition, Matching, Full Disclosure, Consistency, Conservatism, Materiality, and Objectivity.

competency_groups.title: Accounting Process Competencies
competency_groups.description: Competencies derived from Unit-2: Accounting Process covering transaction recording and bookkeeping.
competencies: Analyze the effect of business transactions on assets, liabilities, capital, revenue, and expenses using the accounting equation approach.
Prepare source documents and vouchers for various trade discounts, freight, cartage, and GST scenarios.
Record business transactions in Books of Original Entry including Journal (with special entries), Cash Book (Simple, Bank column, Petty cashbook) and Special Purpose Books.
